content.text:
Cooperative Information Systems
Semester:
2  |
Type:
UEF22  |
VHS:
42h  |
Weekly hours:
C: 1h30 / TD: 1h30  |
Personal work VHS:
21h  |
Coefficient:
3  |
Credits:
4
Teaching Objectives
Understand the fundamental concepts of cooperative information systems (CIS)
Master the architectures and key technologies of CIS
Develop skills in CIS design, development and management
Analyse collaboration and communication challenges in CIS
Evaluate the impact of CIS on organisations and individuals
Recommended prior knowledge:
Project management, databases, information systems.
Course Content (Lectures: 21h)
Cooperative Information Systems
Foundations of distributed information systems: aspects, characteristics
Definition and principles of collaboration and coordination
Challenges and issues of CIS in various contexts
CIS Architecture
Cooperative work and its forms and modalities
Architecture models for CIS (group-oriented, reference architectures, standards-based)
Cooperative work tools (synchronous groupware, asynchronous groupware, DMS, workflow)
CIS Engineering
CIS development methodologies (agile, OSSAD, etc.)
Needs analysis and business process modelling
Collaborative user interface design
Project management and change management in CIS
CIS security
Collaboration and Communication in CIS
Sociological analysis of cooperative/collaborative work
Synchronous and asynchronous communication tools (chat, videoconferencing, etc.)
Knowledge and shared information management
Decision support tools (GDSS, …)
Social aspects of CIS
Assessment
In-person semester exam (60%)
Continuous assessment (CC) (40%): continuous tests, personal work
References
Alquier & al, (2000).
Les systèmes d'information coopératifs : une approche par les collecticiels
.
Baghdadi Y. (1997).
Contribution méthodologique à la conception des systèmes d'information coopératifs
. Thèse de doctorat. Toulouse 1.
Nurcan, Selmin (1996). Analyse et conception de systèmes d'information coopératifs. TSI'96.
Vincent Couturier (2004).
L'ingénierie des systèmes d'information coopératifs par réutilisation
. Thèse de doctorat, Lyon 3.
Van Der Aalst, Kees Van Hee (2002).
Workflow Management: Models, Methods and Systems
. MIT Press.
